title: hieracium caesium subsp. sendtneri
intro: Hieracium caesium subsp. sendtneri, also known as Alpine Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium caesium plant, a flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or made into a tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies to support the immune system and reduce the severity of colds and flu. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and health benefits of Hieracium caesium subsp. sendtneri, but it has shown promise in traditional herbal medicine.
